provisions.title: Issue of treasury bills
provisions.content: Notwithstanding section 22 of the Public Finance Act, the Minister may borrow, by the issue in Uganda of Government treasury bills at any one time or by such installments as he or she may think necessary a sum not exceeding in total twenty-two billion shillings or such further sums as may from time to time be authorised by resolution of Parliament.

provisions.title: Proceeds from issue of treasury bills
provisions.content: The proceeds from the issue of treasury bills shall be paid into the Consolidated Fund.

provisions.title: Principal monies charged upon Consolidated Fund
provisions.content: The principal monies represented by the treasury bills issued under this Act are charged upon and shall be payable out of the Consolidated Fund.

provisions.title: Value of bills when payable
provisions.content: Notwithstanding section 22(3) of the Public Finance Act, every treasury bill issued under this Act shall be for a sum of ten thousand shillings or a multiple of ten thousand shillings and shall be payable at par at such time or times as the Minister shall, before the issue of the treasury bills, fix and determine, but not later than one year from the date of issue.

provisions.title: Rules
provisions.content: The Minister may make rules providing for all or any of the following— (a) the form in which treasury bills shall be issued; (b) the method by which treasury bills shall be issued; (c) the terms upon which treasury bills shall be issued; (d) for discount purposes and any other things or matters necessary for the management of this borrowing.

provisions.title: Bank of Uganda
provisions.content: The Minister may appoint the Bank of Uganda as agent for the Government for any of the purposes of this Act.

provisions.title: Validation
provisions.content: Any treasury bills issued prior to the commencement of this Act, which were in excess of the legal limit then existing under section 2 of the Treasury Bills Act, Cap. 153 , 1964 Revision, shall be deemed to have been validly issued under this Act.

provisions.title: Repayment of treasury bills
provisions.content: The principal monies represented by the treasury bills issued under this Act shall be repaid by the Bank of Uganda, and upon that repayment the treasury bills shall be cancelled by the Bank of Uganda. History of this document 31 December 2000 this version Consolidation 26 September 1969 Commenced Related documents [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Facebook Page Visit our Twitter/X profile About ULII ULII is a project of the Law Reporting Committee of the Judiciary of Uganda. ULII aims to bridge the existing gap in public access to the law of Uganda. Working with the AfricanLII and in collaboration with other partners, such as the Free Access to Law Movement, ULII's major aim is to be a user-friendly portal of all publicly available legal information in Uganda. The Judiciary of Uganda considers access to its decisions and the law upon which they are based as key tenets of its key mission: Justice for All . ULII is also an end user interface for the Electronic Court Case Management Information System.
